notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix ‚Üí /nw-deliver. RCA at bugfix/rca.md is the authoritative specification. User has explicitly confirmed: fail-fast semantics on terminal-during-multi-replica-convergence (Q1+Q2 in RCA ¬ß10), Service-only scope (Q3 in RCA ¬ß10), and option (A) threading shape (RCA ¬ß6 ‚Äî pass `replicas_desired: NonZeroU32` from the handler).

Single-step bugfix landing GREEN test + fix in the SAME commit per RCA ¬ß8.5 and .claude/rules/testing.md ¬ß "RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits". The RED state is documented by GitHub issue #140's body ‚Äî no separate RED scaffold commit is warranted for a focused one-file production change. The new acceptance test sits alongside the existing `s_cp_01_*` family in `streaming_submit.rs`.

Service-only scope per RCA ¬ß4. Job streaming was moved off `ConvergedRunning` semantics by ADR-0047 ¬ß3 [D7] and uses `build_workload_stream` / typed `JobSubmitEvent` enum (no `ConvergedRunning` variant). `ServiceSubmitEvent::ConvergedRunning` at `streaming.rs:1020` is declared but has zero emission sites today; if a future slice wires its emission, the same gate logic applies ‚Äî out of scope for this fix per RCA ¬ß9.

Threading shape: option (A) ‚Äî pass `replicas_desired: NonZeroU32` from the submit handler at `handlers.rs:476` (Service branch) through `build_stream`, `check_terminal`, and `lagged_recover`. The handler ALREADY has the validated `ServiceV1` aggregate in scope (with `replicas: NonZeroU32`) before invoking `build_stream`; extracting via `WorkloadIntent::Service(s) => s.replicas` is one parameter on three function signatures and zero new I/O. Option (B) (one-shot IntentStore hydrate inside the streaming task) was rejected per RCA ¬ß6 ‚Äî adds redundant I/O, a new decode failure mode (`Job::from_store_bytes`), and a coupling between streaming and the codec module that was deliberately kept out.

`lagged_recover` requires more than a parameter addition. The current single-row shortcut (`latest.state == Running`) becomes an aggregate count over all rows belonging to `workload_id`, and the wire-event `alloc_id` / `started_at` must come from the most-recently-updated Running row (not `latest`, which may itself be in a non-Running transition while the count threshold has already been met by sibling rows). See RCA ¬ß6 ad-hoc code block for the exact shape.

Fail-fast semantics on terminal-during-convergence preserved. The terminal-projection branches in `check_terminal` (BackoffExhausted / Stopped / Custom) fire unconditionally BEFORE the running-count gate ‚Äî any single alloc's terminal condition closes the stream regardless of how many siblings are pending or Running. This is the conservative choice per RCA ¬ß7 Q1 recommendation and the user's confirmation: an operator running `submit --wait` against a Service with `replicas: 3` whose one alloc has exhausted its restart budget wants `ConvergedFailed` now, not aggregated outcome after a timeout. The aggregate-outcome design (option (b) in RCA ¬ß7) is a separate concern and explicitly out of scope.

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md`: the old single-row shortcut is DELETED; no fallback path for `replicas_desired = 1` (the count comparison is correct for the single-replica case by construction ‚Äî `running_count >= 1`). No backwards-compat shims, no `#[deprecated]` aliases, no two-phase rollout. Per RCA ¬ß9 the wire-protocol surface (`SubmitEvent` variants) is unchanged ‚Äî only the *when* they fire changes.

TODO(#140) deferral marker at `streaming.rs:358` MUST be removed in the same commit. This fix IS closing #140; carrying a forward-pointer comment after landing the fix violates `CLAUDE.md` ¬ß "Deferrals require GitHub issues" (the deferral language must be dropped once the work lands).

Clippy warnings encountered during the fix are in-scope fixes per `CLAUDE.md` ¬ß "Deferrals require GitHub issues" and `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer.md` ‚Äî fix in the current commit, do NOT classify as pre-existing or create tracking issues.

Trait-method-contract discipline per .claude/rules/development.md ¬ß "Trait definitions specify behavior, not just signature": the new docstrings on `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` MUST state the multi-replica observable behavior (preconditions on `replicas_desired`, postconditions on when `ConvergedRunning` fires, edge case for `running_count >= replicas_desired.get()`, invariant that terminal-projection bypasses the gate).

reviews.scope: RCA + threading shape (option A) + Service-only scope confirmation + fail-fast terminal semantics (Q1+Q2 ‚Üí recommendation (a)).
reviews.notes: User confirmed root cause (Service streaming lane's `check_terminal` / `lagged_recover` emit `ConvergedRunning` on first Running row regardless of `replicas_desired`); approved threading shape (option A ‚Äî pass `replicas_desired: NonZeroU32` from handler through `build_stream`); approved fail-fast semantics on terminal-during-convergence (any single alloc's terminal condition closes the stream); confirmed Service-only scope (Job kind structurally exempt per ADR-0047; `ServiceSubmitEvent::ConvergedRunning` has zero current emission sites).

phases.name: Gate ConvergedRunning on running_count >= replicas_desired
phases.description: Single-step cohesive bugfix landing test + production fix in one commit. Production changes to `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/streaming.rs`: (1) `build_stream` gains a `replicas_desired: NonZeroU32` parameter, threaded into the streaming task closure so both `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` see it; (2) `check_terminal` gains the same parameter; its Running-detection branch replaces `rows.iter().any(|r| r.state == Running)` with an aggregate count `running_count: u32 = rows.iter().filter(...).count().try_into().unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` and emits `ConvergedRunning` only when `running_count >= replicas_desired.get()`; (3) `lagged_recover` gains the same parameter; its non-terminal projection switches from single-row inspection (`latest.state == Running`) to an aggregate count over all rows belonging to `workload_id`, and when the threshold is met, picks the most-recently-updated Running row as the source of `alloc_id` / `started_at` for the wire event (per RCA ¬ß6 ‚Äî `latest` may be a non-Running transition while the count has already met the threshold via sibling rows); (4) the `TODO(#140)` comment at `streaming.rs:358` is REMOVED (deferral marker dropped per `CLAUDE.md` ¬ß "Deferrals require GitHub issues"); (5) `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` docstrings updated to state the multi-replica observable contract per .claude/rules/development.md ¬ß "Trait definitions specify behavior, not just signature". Production changes to `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs:476` (Service branch): extract `replicas_desired = match &intent { WorkloadIntent::Service(s) => s.replicas, _ => unreachable!("Schedule rejected at submit; Job uses build_workload_stream") }` and pass to `build_stream`. New acceptance test appended to `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/streaming_submit.rs` (alongside the existing `s_cp_01_*` family) per the RED-assertion sequence in RCA ¬ß8 ‚Äî a Service spec with `replicas: 2` submitted via the streaming lane, first alloc reaches Running and the stream emits NO `kind == "converged_running"` line, second alloc reaches Running and the stream emits `ConvergedRunning` and the request completes. The test lands GREEN against the fixed implementation in this single commit.

steps.name: gate ConvergedRunning on running_count >= replicas_desired
steps.description: Single cohesive commit landing the production fix + the regression test together. Production edits to `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/streaming.rs` (three function signatures): (1) `pub fn build_stream(state: AppState, workload_id: WorkloadId, accepted: SubmitEvent, replicas_desired: NonZeroU32) -> impl Stream<...>` ‚Äî new `replicas_desired` parameter threaded into the streaming task's closure so subsequent `check_terminal` / `lagged_recover` calls see it. (2) `async fn check_terminal(obs: &dyn ObservationStore, workload_id: &WorkloadId, event: &LifecycleEvent, replicas_desired: NonZeroU32) -> Option<SubmitEvent>` ‚Äî Running-detection branch replaces `let has_running = rows.iter().any(|r| r.workload_id == *workload_id && r.state == AllocState::Running)` with `let running_count: u32 = rows.iter().filter(|r| r.workload_id == *workload_id && r.state == AllocState::Running).count().try_into().unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` and gates the `SubmitEvent::ConvergedRunning` emission on `running_count >= replicas_desired.get()`. Terminal-projection branch (BackoffExhausted / Stopped / Custom) is UNCHANGED ‚Äî fires unconditionally BEFORE the running-count gate per RCA ¬ß7 fail-fast semantics. (3) `async fn lagged_recover(obs: &dyn ObservationStore, workload_id: &WorkloadId, replicas_desired: NonZeroU32) -> Option<SubmitEvent>` ‚Äî non-terminal projection switches from `match latest.state { AllocState::Running => Some(ConvergedRunning { alloc_id: latest.alloc_id, started_at: ... }) }` to: filter all rows belonging to `workload_id` into `job_rows`, count Running rows in `job_rows`, and when `running_count >= replicas_desired.get()` pick the most-recently-updated Running row (`job_rows.iter().filter(|r| r.state == AllocState::Running).max_by_key(|r| r.updated_at.counter)`) as the source of `alloc_id` and `started_at` for the wire event. The terminal-projection arm of `lagged_recover` (if `latest.terminal.is_some()`) is UNCHANGED. (4) The `TODO(#140)` comment at `streaming.rs:358` is REMOVED in the same commit ‚Äî this fix closes #140, and the deferral language must be dropped per `CLAUDE.md` ¬ß "Deferrals require GitHub issues". (5) `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` rustdoc updated to state the multi-replica observable behavior per .claude/rules/development.md ¬ß "Trait definitions specify behavior, not just signature": preconditions (`replicas_desired` is the workload's desired replica count, validated NonZero at construction), postconditions (`ConvergedRunning` is returned only when at least `replicas_desired` rows are in `AllocState::Running` for `workload_id`), edge case (`replicas_desired == 1` behaves identically to the prior single-row shortcut ‚Äî no fallback path needed by construction), invariant (terminal-projection bypasses the gate; any single terminal row closes the stream regardless of sibling progress). Production edit to `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs:476` (Service branch): extract `let replicas_desired = match &intent { WorkloadIntent::Service(s) => s.replicas, _ => unreachable!("Schedule rejected at submit at handlers.rs:258-264; Job uses build_workload_stream at handlers.rs:454") };` and pass it as the new fourth argument to `crate::streaming::build_stream(state.clone(), workload_id.clone(), accepted, replicas_desired)`. Test edit to `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/streaming_submit.rs`: append one new `#[tokio::test]` named `streaming_lane_does_not_emit_converged_running_until_running_count_meets_replicas_desired` per RCA ¬ß8 RED-assertion sequence ‚Äî submit a Service spec with `replicas: 2` via the streaming lane (Accept: application/x-ndjson), wait for `state.lifecycle_events.receiver_count() >= 1` (handler subscribed), inject first allocation reaching Running (`obs.write_alloc_status_row(...)` with `state: Running` + `emit_lifecycle(state, LifecycleEvent { from: Pending, to: Running, alloc_id: "alloc-payments-0", workload_id: "payments", terminal: None })`), assert NO line with `kind == "converged_running"` is in the body yet AND the request task has not completed, inject second allocation reaching Running (`alloc-payments-1`), assert the body NOW contains a `kind == "converged_running"` line and the request task completes within a short timeout. The test reuses the existing `build_app_state` / `build_router` / `emit_lifecycle` / `body_ndjson_lines` / `SimClock` harness ‚Äî no new harness primitive needed per RCA ¬ß8. A new helper `payments_service_spec()` returning a `ServiceSpecInput { replicas: 2, ... }` is added alongside the existing `payments_spec()` (analogous shape, Service variant). Test lands GREEN against the fixed implementation in this single commit. Single-cut migration: no backwards-compat shim for `replicas_desired == 1`; the count comparison is correct for the single-replica case by construction (`running_count >= 1` matches the prior `any(...)` semantics exactly when one replica is desired). Commit message: `fix(streaming): gate ConvergedRunning on running_count >= replicas_desired (closes #140)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: Production changes (single cohesive commit): (1) `build_stream`, `check_terminal`, `lagged_recover` in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/streaming.rs` each carry a new `replicas_desired: NonZeroU32` parameter; (2) `check_terminal` Running-detection branch counts `running_count: u32` over rows matching `workload_id` AND `state == AllocState::Running` and gates `SubmitEvent::ConvergedRunning` emission on `running_count >= replicas_desired.get()`; (3) `lagged_recover` non-terminal projection counts Running rows over `job_rows` (the workload's row subset) and when `>= replicas_desired.get()` picks the most-recently-updated Running row as the wire-event source ‚Äî NOT `latest` (which may itself be a non-Running transition); (4) `lagged_recover` terminal-projection arm and `check_terminal` terminal-projection branch remain UNCHANGED ‚Äî fail-fast semantics preserved per RCA ¬ß7; (5) `TODO(#140)` comment at `streaming.rs:358` is REMOVED; (6) docstrings on `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` state the multi-replica observable contract (preconditions, postconditions, edge cases, invariant about terminal bypassing the gate); (7) `handlers.rs:476` Service branch extracts `replicas_desired` from `WorkloadIntent::Service(s).replicas` and passes to `build_stream`; the unreachable arm for non-Service kinds carries the citation `handlers.rs:258-264` (Schedule rejected at submit) / `handlers.rs:454` (Job uses `build_workload_stream`). Test verification: (8) New `#[tokio::test] streaming_lane_does_not_emit_converged_running_until_running_count_meets_replicas_desired` in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/streaming_submit.rs` passes GREEN ‚Äî first Running row ‚Üí no `kind == "converged_running"` line emitted; second Running row ‚Üí `ConvergedRunning` line emitted and request completes. (9) All pre-existing tests in `streaming_submit.rs` and the workspace remain GREEN. (10) Single-cut: `git grep 'TODO(#140)' crates/` returns no matches; `git grep -n 'has_running' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/streaming.rs` returns no matches (the single-row shortcut is GONE); no `#[deprecated]` aliases. Commit message: `fix(streaming): gate ConvergedRunning on running_count >= replicas_desired (closes #140)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
acceptance_criteria: RCA ¬ß8 RED-assertion sequence ‚Äî a Service spec with `replicas == 2` submitted via the streaming lane (Accept: application/x-ndjson) ‚Äî when the FIRST allocation reaches Running, the stream emits ZERO lines with `kind == "converged_running"` AND the request task has not yet completed.

RCA ¬ß8 RED-assertion sequence ‚Äî when the SECOND allocation reaches Running, the stream emits a `kind == "converged_running"` line AND the request task completes within a short timeout (the existing harness's nominal converged-running timeout).

RCA ¬ß6 `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` both gate on `running_count >= replicas_desired.get()` (NonZeroU32 ‚Äî `.get()` yields the underlying `u32`). The count is computed via `rows.iter().filter(...).count().try_into().unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` against rows matching `workload_id == *workload_id && state == AllocState::Running`. The replaced single-row shortcut (`rows.iter().any(...)` in `check_terminal`; `match latest.state` in `lagged_recover`) is GONE.

RCA ¬ß6 `lagged_recover` non-terminal projection picks the most-recently-updated Running row (`job_rows.iter().filter(|r| r.state == Running).max_by_key(|r| r.updated_at.counter)`) as the source of `alloc_id` / `started_at` for the wire event ‚Äî NOT `latest` (which may itself be a non-Running transition while the count threshold has already been met by sibling rows).

RCA ¬ß7 fail-fast semantics preserved ‚Äî terminal-projection branches in `check_terminal` (BackoffExhausted / Stopped / Custom) fire unconditionally BEFORE the running-count gate; any single alloc's terminal condition closes the stream regardless of sibling progress. With `replicas: 3` and 2 Running + 1 BackoffExhausted, the stream emits `ConvergedFailed { BackoffExhausted }` and ends (current behaviour preserved). Same applies to `Stopped { by }` and `Custom { ... }` per user confirmation of Q1+Q2.

Trait-contract discipline per .claude/rules/development.md ¬ß "Trait definitions specify behavior, not just signature" ‚Äî the rustdoc on `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` states preconditions (`replicas_desired` is the workload's desired replica count), postconditions (when `ConvergedRunning` is returned), edge cases (`replicas_desired == 1` behaves identically to the prior single-row shortcut by construction; no backwards-compat shim needed), and the invariant that terminal-projection bypasses the gate.

Service-only scope ‚Äî the fix touches only the legacy flat `SubmitEvent::ConvergedRunning` path on the Service streaming lane. No edits to `JobSubmitEvent` (no `ConvergedRunning` variant per ADR-0047 ¬ß3 [D7]), no edits to `ServiceSubmitEvent::ConvergedRunning` (declared at `streaming.rs:1020` with zero emission sites ‚Äî out of scope per RCA ¬ß9).

TODO(#140) deferral marker at `streaming.rs:358` is REMOVED per `CLAUDE.md` ¬ß "Deferrals require GitHub issues" ‚Äî this fix closes #140; carrying the forward-pointer after landing the fix is forbidden.

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md` ‚Äî no backwards-compat fallback for `replicas_desired == 1` (the count comparison is correct for the single-replica case by construction); no `#[deprecated]` aliases; no two-phase rollout.

`integration-tests` feature gate per .claude/rules/testing.md ¬ß "Integration vs unit gating" ‚Äî the new acceptance test sits in `tests/acceptance/streaming_submit.rs`, which is already gated behind the existing feature; no new gate is introduced.
Clippy warnings encountered during the fix are fixed in-scope per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer.md`, NOT classified as pre-existing and deferred.
Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer and `closes #140`; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
steps.tier: Tier 1 (acceptance ‚Äî `integration-tests` feature, runs through `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --test acceptance --features integration-tests`). Pure in-process ‚Äî uses the existing `build_app_state` / `build_router` / `emit_lifecycle` harness with `SimClock` and `SimDriver`; no real cgroups, no real network, no subprocess.
quality_gates: TODO(#140) deferral marker at `streaming.rs:358` is REMOVED in the same commit ‚Äî verify via `git grep 'TODO(#140)' crates/` returning zero matches.

Integration acceptance lane green in Lima VM: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --test acceptance --features integration-tests` exits 0; the new `streaming_lane_does_not_emit_converged_running_until_running_count_meets_replicas_desired` test passes alongside the existing `s_cp_01_*` family with zero regressions.

Compile-only gate on macOS per .claude/rules/development.md ¬ß "Compile-checking": `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --no-run --features integration-tests` exits 0 (run through `cargo xtask lima run --` when invoked on macOS, per the same rule's Lima requirement for `--features integration-tests`; direct invocation also acceptable since `--no-run` is allowed by the bare-nextest hook).

Clippy clean: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo clippy -p overdrive-control-plane -- -D warnings` exits 0. Any warnings encountered during the fix are resolved in-scope, NOT classified as pre-existing per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer.md`.
Workspace default lane green: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0 (confirms the change does not regress the default unit/proptest lane).

dst-lint clean: `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 ‚Äî the fix does not introduce any banned API (no `Instant::now()`, no `SystemTime::now()`, no raw `tokio::net::*`) into `core` compile paths; `streaming.rs` is in the `overdrive-control-plane` adapter crate, not `core`, but the gate still scans for accidental cross-pollination.

Single-cut verification: `git grep -n 'has_running' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/streaming.rs` returns no matches (the deleted single-row shortcut is gone); `git grep 'TODO(#140)' crates/` returns no matches; no `#[deprecated]` aliases introduced.

Production-edit precision: `git diff HEAD~1 -- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/streaming.rs` shows changes confined to the three named function signatures (`build_stream`, `check_terminal`, `lagged_recover`) + the rustdoc updates + the TODO removal; no incidental edits to unrelated `streaming.rs` regions (the `JobSubmitEvent` / `ServiceSubmitEvent` type definitions are untouched per RCA ¬ß4 Service-only scope).

`git diff HEAD~1 -- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs` shows exactly the Service-branch extraction at `handlers.rs:476` plus the call-site update ‚Äî no edits to the Job branch (`handlers.rs:454`), no edits to the Schedule rejection at `handlers.rs:258-264`.
Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer and `closes #140`; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
deliverables: Single cohesive commit landing: (a) `replicas_desired: NonZeroU32` parameter added to `build_stream`, `check_terminal`, `lagged_recover` in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/streaming.rs`; (b) count-based emission gate in `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` replacing the prior single-row shortcuts; (c) `lagged_recover` picks the most-recently-updated Running row for the wire event when the threshold is met; (d) `TODO(#140)` comment at `streaming.rs:358` removed; (e) rustdoc on `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` updated per trait-contract discipline; (f) `handlers.rs:476` extracts `replicas_desired` from the validated `WorkloadIntent::Service(s)` and passes to `build_stream`; (g) new `#[tokio::test]` `streaming_lane_does_not_emit_converged_running_until_running_count_meets_replicas_desired` appended to `streaming_submit.rs`.
steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping for this single-step cohesive landing (GREEN test + fix in one commit per RCA ¬ß8.5 and .claude/rules/testing.md ¬ß "RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits"; the RED state is documented by GH issue #140's body ‚Äî no separate RED scaffold commit is warranted for a focused one-file production change). PREPARE ‚Äî re-read RCA ¬ß6 for the verbatim signature shapes (`replicas_desired: NonZeroU32` on all three functions), ¬ß6 ad-hoc code blocks for `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` body shape, ¬ß7 for fail-fast terminal semantics, ¬ß8 for the test RED-assertion sequence, ¬ß9 for the no-staleness invariant (replica count cannot change mid-stream in Phase 1 ‚Äî `ServiceV1` aggregates are immutable once persisted; re-submit with different spec hash is `PutOutcome::Conflict` at `handlers.rs:432-434`). Use Serena's `find_symbol` / `replace_symbol_body` on the three `streaming.rs` functions and the `handlers.rs:476` Service branch ‚Äî the changes are localised to named symbols. Confirm `NonZeroU32` is in scope in both files (likely re-exported from `overdrive-core`; add `use std::num::NonZeroU32;` or the core re-export if not already imported). RED_ACCEPTANCE ‚Äî write the new `#[tokio::test]` in `streaming_submit.rs` per RCA ¬ß8: build a `payments_service_spec()` helper (analogous to the existing `payments_spec()` but constructing `ServiceSpecInput { replicas: 2, ... }`); submit via the streaming lane (Accept: application/x-ndjson); spawn the request as a `tokio::spawn(...)`; wait for `state.lifecycle_events.receiver_count() >= 1` (handler subscribed); inject first row + lifecycle event; yield/advance SimClock by a small Œµ; assert `body_ndjson_lines(...)` contains zero lines with `kind == "converged_running"` and the request task is still pending; inject second row + lifecycle event; assert the body now contains a `kind == "converged_running"` line and the task completes within the harness's nominal timeout. RED_UNIT ‚Äî N/A (the body changes in `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` are inline; no new helper functions). GREEN ‚Äî implement in this order: (1) `streaming.rs`: add `replicas_desired: NonZeroU32` parameter to `build_stream`, `check_terminal`, `lagged_recover` signatures; capture `replicas_desired` into the streaming task closure (by value ‚Äî `NonZeroU32: Copy`); (2) `check_terminal`: replace the `has_running = rows.iter().any(...)` line with the count-based gate per RCA ¬ß6 ad-hoc code block; terminal-projection branch UNCHANGED; (3) `lagged_recover`: replace the single-row inspection with the aggregate count + most-recently-updated Running row selection per RCA ¬ß6 ad-hoc code block; terminal-projection arm UNCHANGED; (4) `streaming.rs:358`: REMOVE the `TODO(#140): gate ConvergedRunning on running_count >= replicas_desired ...` comment (the deferral marker is now satisfied); (5) update rustdoc on `check_terminal` and `lagged_recover` to state the multi-replica observable contract (preconditions on `replicas_desired`, postconditions on `ConvergedRunning` emission, edge case for `replicas_desired == 1`, invariant on terminal-projection bypassing the gate) per .claude/rules/development.md ¬ß "Trait definitions specify behavior, not just signature"; (6) `handlers.rs:476` Service branch: extract `let replicas_desired = match &intent { WorkloadIntent::Service(s) => s.replicas, _ => unreachable!("Schedule rejected at submit at handlers.rs:258-264; Job uses build_workload_stream at handlers.rs:454") };` and pass as the new fourth argument to `build_stream`. (7) Run `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` ‚Äî should compile. (8) Run `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --test acceptance --features integration-tests` ‚Äî the new test passes alongside the existing `s_cp_01_*` family with zero regressions. (9) Run `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --no-run --features integration-tests` (compile-only on macOS per .claude/rules/development.md ¬ß "Compile-checking") ‚Äî should pass. (10) Run `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo clippy -p overdrive-control-plane -- -D warnings` ‚Äî clean; any warnings surfaced are fixed in-scope per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer.md`, not deferred. (11) Run `cargo xtask dst-lint` ‚Äî clean. (12) Run `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` ‚Äî workspace-relative default lane green. COMMIT ‚Äî `git commit` (no `--no-verify`); commit message: `fix(streaming): gate ConvergedRunning on running_count >= replicas_desired (closes #140)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer.
